originalTitle: 'The weapons were loud, but there was always music': Sudanese band play on through the war
title: Music Power! A Brave Band's Song in Sudan!
content:
Hello from Your Kid Reporter!

Hi everyone! It's me, Leo, your kid-friendly news reporter for Africa! I'm so excited to tell you a super special story today. It's a story about music, bravery, and how people can bring joy even when things are tough. Our story takes us to a wonderful country called Sudan. Sudan is in Africa, which is a very big continent with many beautiful places and friendly people. Sometimes, things can be a little bit sad or loud in parts of the world. But guess what? Even then, people find amazing ways to stay strong and make others smile. Today, we're talking about a special group of people who do this with music! Did you know? Africa is the second-largest continent on Earth! It's bigger than just one country; it's a huge land with 54 different countries! This story is about a band that played their music loudly and proudly, even when there were other loud noises around them. Let's meet them!

Meet Aswat Almadina!

Our special band is called Aswat Almadina. That's a fun name to say, isn't it? It means "Voices of the City." How cool is that? They are one of the most popular bands in Sudan. People love their music! Their songs make people want to dance, sing along, and just feel happy. They play cheerful tunes that fill the air with good feelings. Aswat Almadina has many talented musicians. Some play instruments like drums, guitars, and keyboards. Others sing with beautiful voices. Together, they make wonderful music. Did you know? A band is a group of people who play musical instruments or sing together! Just like a team in sports, they work together to make something awesome. They love to make music that tells stories and shares happiness with everyone in their city and beyond. They were just doing what they loved, making music in a special room called a studio.

A Very Loud Day

Imagine this: Three years ago, Aswat Almadina was in their music studio. A studio is a special, quiet room where bands practice and record their songs. They were making beautiful sounds, just like any other day. But then, something very different happened. Suddenly, there were lots of loud noises outside. Boom! Bang! It was a bit scary. These were the sounds of war starting. War is when people fight, and it can make things very noisy and confusing. It's a sad time for everyone. Many people might want to hide or stop what they are doing when things get loud and scary like that. But not Aswat Almadina! They were brave. Did you know? Even when things are scary, some people find their courage and keep doing good things to help others! Even with the loud noises all around, the band kept going. They kept playing their music! The sounds of their instruments filled their studio, trying to be louder than the scary sounds outside.

Why Music is Magic

Why did they keep playing? Because music is magic! Music has a special power. It can make you feel many things. It can make you feel happy when you're sad. It can make you feel brave when you're scared. It can help you forget about worries for a little while and just enjoy the moment. When things are tough, like during a war, people need hope. They need something to make them smile, even for a moment. Music can do that! Did you know? Music has been important to people for thousands of years! People all over the world use music to celebrate, tell stories, and comfort each other. The members of Aswat Almadina knew that their music could be like a warm hug for people. They knew their songs could give strength and a little bit of peace to their friends and family. Their music was a way to say, "Don't give up!"

Singing for Hope

So, even when the war was happening, the band kept making music. They recorded new songs. They shared their music with everyone they could. Their melodies became a message of hope. They sang about peace, about love, and about their beautiful country. They wanted everyone to remember the good things, even during the hard times. Their songs were like a light shining in the dark. Imagine listening to a beautiful song when you feel worried. It can make you feel a little bit better, right? That's what Aswat Almadina's music did for many people in Sudan. Did you know? Instruments like drums and guitars can be found in almost every country's music! They help create different sounds and feelings in songs. They showed everyone that even though the weapons were loud, their music was louder in a different way. It was louder with kindness, hope, and the power of people coming together.

What They Do Now

Today, Aswat Almadina is still making music! They continue to play their wonderful songs. They want to inspire everyone to keep hoping for a better tomorrow. They travel and share their music with more people. They remind us all that art, like music, can be a very powerful thing. It helps people to heal and to dream of happier days. Their story teaches us that even when big, scary things happen, we can find ways to be brave and share love. We can use our talents to help others and bring light into the world. Did you know? Many musicians all around Africa use their music to tell important stories about their culture and history! This brave band from Sudan shows us how strong the human spirit can be. They use their voices and instruments to build bridges and spread happiness, one song at a time.

A Message of Peace

Aswat Almadina hopes for peace to come to Sudan and to every part of the world. They want everyone to be safe, happy, and able to enjoy music and play together. Their story is a wonderful reminder that kindness and art can shine through any darkness. Music helps us remember to be good to each other and to always look for the good things in life. So, let's remember Aswat Almadina and their brave music. Let's remember the power of a song to bring hope, even when the world is loud. Keep singing, Aswat Almadina! Did you know? Listening to music can make your brain happy and even help you learn new things! Thank you for listening to this amazing story from Africa. Until next time, keep spreading joy and kindness!
summary: Aswat Almadina, a popular band from Sudan, bravely kept making music even when war broke out three years ago, using their songs to bring hope and happiness to people during tough times.
whyMatters: This story is important because it shows us that even when things are scary and loud, like a war, music and art can be very powerful. It teaches us that people can be brave and use their talents to spread hope, joy, and peace to others, helping everyone feel a little bit better.

title: US intercepts and seizes Iranian-flagged cargo ship, Trump says
content: Tehran has not commented on the incident, which comes as the US prepares for a second round of talks.

originalTitle: Eight children killed in Louisiana shooting, police say
title: Sad News from Far, Far Away, But You Are Safe!
content:
Hello, wonderful friends! Your friendly reporter from Africa is here. Sometimes, news can be a little bit sad. It's like when a toy breaks, or when a friend moves far away. Today, we have some sad news from a place very, very far away from our beautiful Africa. It happened in a country called America, in a town named Shreveport in Louisiana. We know it can be a bit scary or confusing to hear sad news. But please know that your grown-ups, like your parents, teachers, and guardians, are always working hard to keep you safe and loved. This sad story is from very far away, and we want you to feel safe and happy wherever you are in Africa.

Sad News from Far Away

In a place called Louisiana, which is in a country called America, something very, very sad happened. Imagine a place with lots of homes and streets, just like where you live, but across a huge ocean. In one of those homes, a very big problem happened. Eight children, who were just like you – some were tiny babies only one year old, and some were a bit older, up to 14 years old – lost their lives. This means they are no longer with us. It is a very, very sad event that makes many people feel heartbroken. We are sending our biggest hugs and kind thoughts to everyone who is sad because of this. Did you know? The world is a very big place! Africa is a continent, and America is another continent, far across the Atlantic Ocean. It takes a very, very long time to travel from Africa to America!

What Happened?

The police, who are grown-ups whose job it is to help people and keep everyone safe, said this sad thing happened because of a 'domestic disturbance.' That's a big grown-up word, but we can explain it simply for you. It means there was a very serious problem between grown-ups who live together in a home. When grown-ups have big arguments or disagreements, sometimes things can become very difficult and sad. This time, the problems became so big that it hurt innocent children. It's important to remember that grown-ups should always try to solve their problems calmly and kindly, and always, always protect children. Children should always feel safe and loved in their homes.

Understanding Big Words

Let's talk a little more about 'domestic disturbance.' Think of it like this: 'Domestic' means things happening inside a home or family. And 'disturbance' means a big problem or trouble. So, a 'domestic disturbance' is when there is a big problem or fight inside a family's home. Sometimes, grown-ups need help to solve their problems. There are special grown-ups who can help families talk and find peaceful ways to be together. It's always best to talk things out and be kind to each other. Did you know? Communication is a big word for talking and listening! When people communicate well, they can solve many problems without getting angry or sad.

It's Okay to Feel

Hearing about something so sad can make you feel many different things. You might feel a little bit sad, or confused, or even a bit worried. And guess what? All those feelings are okay! Grown-ups feel sad and worried too when they hear such news. It's like when you watch a sad movie, and you feel tears in your eyes. That's a normal feeling. What's most important is to talk about how you feel. Don't keep those feelings inside your heart. Share them with a grown-up you trust. Your mom, dad, grandmother, grandfather, auntie, uncle, or your teacher would love to hear what's on your mind. They can give you a big hug and help you understand things better. Talking helps our hearts feel lighter.

You Are Safe in Africa

We want to remind you again, this sad event happened very, very far away from our homes in Africa. Many, many lands and a very big ocean are between us and Louisiana. Your homes here in Africa are safe. Your parents and the grown-ups who care for you work very hard every day to make sure you are protected, happy, and loved. They make sure you have food to eat, a warm bed to sleep in, and a safe place to play and learn. Always remember that your safety is the most important thing to the grown-ups in your life. They love you more than words can say. Did you know? There are millions and millions of children living in Africa, just like you, playing and learning every day! Grown-ups all over Africa care very much for their children.

Grown-Ups Keep Us Safe

From the moment you are born, grown-ups work hard to keep you safe. They teach you how to cross the road, how to be careful when playing, and how to stay away from danger. They are like your personal superheroes, always watching out for you. If you ever feel unsafe, or if you ever see something that makes you worried, the most important thing to do is tell a grown-up immediately. They are there to listen and to help. You are never alone. Always remember that good grown-ups want to hear what you have to say. Your voice is important.

Being Kind and Peaceful

Even though this sad news happened far away, it reminds us all about how important it is to be kind to each other. In our homes, at school, and in our communities, we can all try our best to be peaceful. Being kind means sharing our toys, helping a friend, using gentle words, and being respectful to everyone. When we are kind, we make our corner of the world a happier and safer place for everyone, especially for children. Let's try every day to spread kindness and peace. A smile, a helping hand, or a kind word can make a big difference. Did you know? When you are kind to someone, it makes you feel happy inside too! It's like a special superpower that makes everyone feel good.

Sending Love Across the World

So, while we send our thoughts and wishes to the children and families in Louisiana who are hurting, we also want you, our wonderful children in Africa, to feel safe, loved, and happy. Remember to talk to your grown-ups, be kind to your friends, and always know that you are very special and important. Let's all try to make our homes and communities places filled with peace and love, just like a big, warm hug. Thank you for listening, my dear friends. Stay safe and keep shining brightly! We love you very much.

originalTitle: Tehran will never cede control of Strait of Hormuz, senior Iranian politician tells BBC
title: Tehran will never cede control of Strait of Hormuz, senior Iranian politician tells BBC
content: Lyse Doucet speaks to Ebrahim Azizi, who says Iran "will decide the right of passage" through the crucial shipping route.
